execution.commit_title: docs(statsd): fix example metric names to match myapp.* pattern
execution.pr_title: docs(statsd): fix example metric names to match myapp.* pattern
execution.pr_body:
## Summary
Fixes misleading StatsD example metric names in the Basic Configuration Structure section. The documentation previously instructed users to send `foo:10|g` and `bar:20|g`, but the example `[app]` configuration filters on `myapp.*` — so those metrics would never appear in the synthetic chart as described.

## User Request
User reported that the StatsD basic configuration example at learn.netdata.cloud produced nonsensical results because the test metric names (`foo`, `bar`) did not match the app pattern (`myapp.*`), causing the synthetic chart to never populate.

## Reason for Change
The example was internally inconsistent: the sample data did not match the `[app]` metrics pattern, so users following the documentation would not see the described behavior. Updated the metric names to `myapp.metric1` and `myapp.metric2` to match the `myapp.*` pattern, and added a clarifying note about the pattern-matching requirement.

context.user_request: Is the example in https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/collecting-metrics/statsd#basic-configuration-structure out of date? I'm experimenting it and am getting nonsensical results

plan_item.summary: Task class: improve_docs — Fix misleading StatsD example metric names in config docs
plan_item.edit_guidance: In the 'Basic Configuration Structure' section, locate the sentence (around line 499): "When you send metrics like `foo:10|g` and `bar:20|g`, you'll see both private charts and your synthetic chart." Change `foo:10|g` to `myapp.metric1:10|g` and `bar:20|g` to `myapp.metric2:20|g` so the metric names match the `myapp.*` pattern defined in the example's [app] section. This ensures users who follow the instruction will actually see the metrics appear in the synthetic chart as described. Optionally add a brief clarifying note: 'These metric names must match the pattern defined in the [app] section (e.g., myapp.*) for them to appear in your synthetic charts.'
acceptance_checks: The sentence referencing foo:10|g and bar:20|g is updated to use metric names that match the myapp.* pattern (e.g., myapp.metric1:10|g and myapp.metric2:20|g).
The example [app] configuration block (metrics = myapp.*) remains unchanged.
A clarifying note is present stating that metric names must match the [app] metrics pattern to appear in synthetic charts.
No other sections of the README are modified.

context.diff:
diff --git a/src/collectors/statsd.plugin/README.md b/src/collectors/statsd.plugin/README.md index 5581c4161..28923556e 100644 --- a/src/collectors/statsd.plugin/README.md +++ b/src/collectors/statsd.plugin/README.md @@ -496,7 +496,7 @@ For example, to monitor the application `myapp` using StatsD and Netdata, create Using this configuration, `myapp` gets its own dashboard section with one chart containing two [dimensions](https://learn.netdata.cloud/docs/developer-and-contributor-corner/glossary#d). -When you send metrics like `foo:10|g` and `bar:20|g`, you'll see both private charts and your synthetic chart. +When you send metrics like `myapp.metric1:10|g` and `myapp.metric2:20|g`, you'll see both private charts and your synthetic chart. These metric names must match the pattern defined in the `[app]` section (e.g., `myapp.*`) for them to appear in your synthetic charts.
